threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep trying to find a parking spot anywhere near Chatsworth Road but keep circling like it's a game of musical chairs. It's not.
Caught the 333 last night, and a guy serenaded the driver with an opera tune. Even the drunks were impressed, not your usual late-night chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid selling handmade friendship bracelets at the U-Bahn entrance while a street musician played an off-tune rendition of 'Wonderwall.'

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see folks munching on döner while chatting about the latest U-Bahn delay, totally unfazed. Effortlessly casual, it's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can name at least three taquerias that still use cash only. The tourists are still looking for the ATM.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo sprinting after the 5 bus today, tripped on his own bowtie. Only in SF do we dress up to catch a ride on public transit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the street performers in Plaça del Sol are there for the money, but most are just locals jamming after a long week.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they stare at the metro map in confusion instead of just asking someone for directions like the rest of us.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch at the sound of the 106 bus screeching to a halt, thinking it’s an emergency. Locals don’t even blink.
The 133 driver last night was singing along to the radio like it was a concert. Passengers loved it, but I swear we missed our stop because of his solo.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I’m really in Neukölln was when I saw the kebab shop owner and the barista at the corner café arguing over whose late-night menu was better.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a Döner at 4 AM post-clubbing and watch street artists turn trash into gallery-worthy pieces on the sidewalk.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always spot a true local—they’ll dodge the tourists at Dolores Park like pros, while the newcomers stand in the middle, lost with maps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the N-Judah coaching a lost tourist on the best burrito spots. Only in SF do strangers bond over tacos on public transport.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the plaça is just for selfies, but locals use it to catch up, sip vermut, and watch the world go by. Totally different vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by how they still treat the metro like it's a sightseeing tour, staring at maps instead of just following the crowd.
